title: What Is Boulder Creek Known For?
st: What Is Boulder Creek, California Known For?
sd: Boulder Creek is known as the gateway to Big Basin, a former lumber boomtown and railroad terminus, a silent-film location, and a town that voted to stop being a city.

title: Boulder Creek History Timeline: From Ancient Redwoods to Today
st: Boulder Creek History Timeline: Every Major Date, Sourced
sd: A dated timeline of Boulder Creek, California: sawmills, the flume, the railroad, Big Basin, the film era, cityhood and its end, floods, fire and recovery.

title: Famous People Who Lived in, Grew Up in or Visited Boulder Creek
st: Famous People From Boulder Creek, California: The Verified List
sd: Who's actually from Boulder Creek? A sourced directory separating people raised here, former residents, working visitors, and claims that don't hold up.

title: Every Movie, Short Film and Music Video Filmed in Boulder Creek
st: Every Movie and Music Video Filmed in Boulder Creek, California
sd: A sourced database of films, shorts and music videos shot in Boulder Creek and Big Basin, from 1914 silent westerns to Vertigo to a Rachel Platten video.

title: Poverty Flat: Boulder Creek's Forgotten Silent-Film Town
st: Poverty Flat: Boulder Creek's Lost Silent-Film Town
sd: In 1915 a film company built a 25-building fake gold-rush town outside Boulder Creek. At least eight silent films were shot there. Then it vanished.

title: Beatriz Michelena: The Silent-Film Pioneer Who Brought Movie Production to Boulder Creek
st: Beatriz Michelena: The Star Who Built a Movie Town Near Boulder Creek
sd: Beatriz Michelena was one of the first Latina stars of American film. In 1915 she co-built a 25-building movie set outside Boulder Creek and made films there.

title: Mary Pickford in Boulder Creek: A Romance of the Redwoods
st: Mary Pickford Filmed in Boulder Creek: A Romance of the Redwoods (1917)
sd: In 1917 Cecil B. DeMille brought Mary Pickford to Boulder Creek to shoot A Romance of the Redwoods on the Poverty Flat set. The film still survives.

title: Why Isn't Boulder Creek a City?
st: Why Isn't Boulder Creek a City? It Was One, and Voted to Quit
sd: Boulder Creek was an incorporated California city until residents voted to disincorporate in 1915. Here's what happened and what unincorporated means today.
